text: Standing here, I feel a gentle sense of closure and transition. The repeated goodbyes echo in my thoughts, giving the end of the event a peculiar weight—like the end of a conversation you wish lingered a bit longer. Now, moving into the exhibit, the philosophical questions about perception and focus draw me in. What do I miss as I leave one space and enter another? There’s a bittersweet curiosity about what remains when we say goodbye—memories, impressions, lingering thoughts, the kinetic energy of departure. I feel both the loss and the pull of new questions as I move onward.
